title: NPS vs PPF — Which to Choose?
meta_description: Compare National Pension System (NPS) and PPF. Returns, tax benefits, withdrawal rules, and which is better for retirement planning in India.

hero.one_liner: Two of India's best retirement savings options compared — understand the trade-offs and pick what suits your goals

sections.heading: Quick Comparison
sections.footnote: NPS returns depend on the fund and allocation chosen. PPF rate is revised quarterly.

sections.heading: When to Choose NPS
content: Choose NPS if you want the additional ₹50,000 tax deduction under Section 80CCD(1B) — this is over and above the ₹1.5 lakh 80C limit. If you're in the 30% tax bracket, this saves ₹15,600 in tax every year.

NPS also makes sense if you want higher potential returns. With up to 75% equity allocation (in the active choice), NPS has historically delivered 9-12% returns — significantly more than PPF's 7.1%. The trade-off is that returns are not guaranteed and the lock-in is until age 60.

sections.heading: When to Choose PPF
content: Choose PPF if you want guaranteed, completely tax-free returns with zero risk. PPF's EEE status means your deposit, interest, and maturity are all tax-free — NPS can't match this since 40% of your NPS corpus must buy an annuity (which is taxable).

PPF is also better if you want flexibility. The 15-year lock-in sounds long, but you can take partial withdrawals from year 7. NPS locks your money until 60 (with limited exceptions). If you're self-employed or don't have a long investment horizon, PPF is simpler.

steps.title: First: PPF up to ₹1.5 lakh/year
steps.description: Max out your 80C deduction with PPF. Guaranteed 7.1% tax-free returns with zero risk. This is your safe base.

steps.title: Then: NPS for extra ₹50K tax saving
steps.description: Invest ₹50,000 in NPS to claim the additional 80CCD(1B) deduction. Choose aggressive allocation (75% equity) since this is long-term.

steps.title: Extra savings: SIP in mutual funds
steps.description: After PPF and NPS, invest any remaining savings in equity mutual fund SIPs. No tax benefit but highest return potential and full liquidity.

faqs.question: Is NPS returns guaranteed?
faqs.answer: No. NPS invests in stocks and bonds, so returns depend on market performance. Historical returns have been 8-12% for equity-heavy allocations. But unlike PPF, there's no guarantee.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw NPS before 60?
faqs.answer: Partial withdrawal (up to 25% of your own contributions) is allowed after 3 years for specific reasons: education, marriage, home purchase, medical emergency. Full premature exit is allowed after 5 years but 80% must buy an annuity.

faqs.question: What is annuity in NPS?
faqs.answer: When you retire, 40% of your NPS corpus must be used to buy an annuity — a monthly pension plan from an insurance company. The remaining 60% can be withdrawn tax-free. The annuity income is taxable at your slab rate.

faqs.question: Which NPS fund should I choose?
faqs.answer: If you're under 40, choose Active Choice with 75% equity (Scheme E), 15% corporate bonds (Scheme C), and 10% government bonds (Scheme G). As you age, gradually reduce equity. Choose a large fund manager like SBI, HDFC, or ICICI.

disclaimer: This article is for educational purposes only. Returns mentioned are historical and not guaranteed. Consult a financial advisor for personalized retirement planning.
